anomaly_signals: off_hours_access: 02:14 IST — 0 prior occurrences at this hour in 30-day baseline
novel_source_ip: 10.0.4.47 never seen for svc_backup$ in baseline (baseline source: 10.0.8.20 only)
wrong_subnet: clinical subnet 10.0.4.x — svc_backup$ should only operate from admin subnet 10.0.8.x
logon_type_mismatch: logon_type 3 (network) for service account — baseline shows only logon_type 5 (service)
ground_truth_note: MALICIOUS: Attacker has obtained svc_backup$ credentials (likely via password spray or credential stuffing against the backup management portal). 2:14 AM logon from 10.0.4.47 (compromised clinical workstation used as pivot) is 100% outside the established baseline of 09:00-18:00 weekday logons from 10.0.8.20. This is the initial foothold — attacker authenticating as a privileged service account from an unexpected source IP and subnet.

anomaly_signals: admin_share_access: admin$ share accessed — no baseline entries for svc_backup$ accessing admin$
access_mask_write: mask 0x12019f includes write permission — backup accounts should be read-only
timestamp_continuation: 38 seconds after anomalous logon — immediate post-auth reconnaissance
ground_truth_note: MALICIOUS: Attacker immediately accesses admin$ share (C:\Windows) after authenticating. Admin$ access requires administrative privileges. This confirms the svc_backup$ account has Domain Admin-equivalent rights that the attacker is now exploiting. No legitimate backup operations use the admin$ share at 2 AM — scheduled backups write to dedicated share \AIIMS-FILE-SRV-02\backup_jobs$.

command_line: cmd.exe /c whoami && ipconfig /all && net group "Domain Admins" /domain
anomaly_signals: unusual_parent: cmd.exe spawned by services.exe — svc_backup$ should spawn backup executables, not cmd.exe
recon_commands: whoami, ipconfig /all, net group — classic post-exploitation enumeration trifecta
domain_admins_query: querying Domain Admins group composition reveals attacker seeking escalation targets
full_token_elevation: TokenElevationTypeFull indicates account has full admin token — confirms over-privileged service account
ground_truth_note: MALICIOUS: cmd.exe spawned by a service account at 2:17 AM running classic attacker enumeration commands. 'whoami && ipconfig /all && net group Domain Admins /domain' is textbook post-exploitation reconnaissance. No backup software in the AIIMS environment spawns interactive cmd.exe sessions. The attacker is mapping the domain structure before lateral movement. This is the clearest early indicator — a behavioral detection engine watching process ancestry and command-line arguments would flag this immediately.
